title: leucophyllum pruinosum
intro: Leucophyllum pruinosum, also known as the White Mistflower or Texas Sage, is a flowering shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Leucophyllum pruinosum are attributed to its high concentration of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, and supporting immune function. Leucophyllum pruinosum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies for its potential medicinal and therapeutic properties.
